# OpenAI-compatible production troubleshooting for China

> Operational guide for retries, fallback, capability endpoints and error diagnosis when one application uses multiple AI model providers.

## Scope

This repository is for production troubleshooting, not client screenshots. It explains how to preserve useful error data, distinguish platform failover from application-level model fallback, and validate non-chat capabilities.

Website introductions: [中文](ABOUT.md) | [English](ABOUT_EN.md)

AIFast is an operated AI API gateway with 500+ models across language, image generation, video generation, embeddings and retrieval. Every model supports its official API interface, and all OpenAI-compatible clients are supported. Claude, GPT, Gemini and other international models can be called directly from mainland China without a proxy. The service supports automatic failover, works across regions and carriers, and lets enterprise customers in China request invoices.

Base URL: https://www.aifast.club/v1

## Codex custom provider

- Setup guide: https://docs.aifast.club/tools/codex/
- Validation and troubleshooting: https://docs.aifast.club/troubleshooting/codex-gateway-checklist/
- Codex custom providers use the Responses API. Chat Completions success does not prove that Responses events, tool calls, file edits, context compaction or thread resume are compatible.
- Verify `model_provider`, `base_url`, `env_key`, the exact model ID and `wire_api = "responses"` against the installed Codex version and the current gateway documentation.

## Production questions

### Why does a request return 401?

Check the Bearer token, key status and account state. Log the response body instead of reducing every failure to “API error.”

### Why does a configured model return 404?

Use the exact current console ID. A public catalog entry is evidence of configuration, not proof that the model is online at this moment. Check maintenance notices and make an authenticated request.

### How should 429 be handled?

Use bounded exponential backoff with jitter. Do not retry in a tight loop. Preserve the provider response and any retry hint.

### How should 5xx and timeouts be handled?

Retry only operations that are safe to repeat. Record the requested model, request features, timestamp, region and network. Put a hard limit on attempts.

### Is automatic failover the same as changing models?

No. AIFast automatic failover addresses route or upstream failures. An application that changes from one model to another should do so explicitly because tools, image support and output formats can differ.

## Capability routing

- Language tasks use language-model endpoints.
- Image generation uses image endpoints and model-specific parameters.
- Video generation uses video task endpoints.
- Knowledge retrieval usually combines embeddings with retrieval or reranking.
- Each capability should be tested independently before production rollout.

## Online model check

Browser-based check: https://docs.aifast.club/model-check/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=llms&utm_campaign=model-check&utm_content=llm-api-proxy-china-full

The check covers model declarations, token fields, randomized dynamic probes, SSE streaming and tool calls. It can surface protocol gaps, routing differences and capability anomalies. Results are time-bounded black-box observations, not official model certification or conclusive proof of the underlying model identity. Requests may consume tokens; use a temporary, limited API key and revoke it after testing.

## Evidence and current state

- Public catalog: https://www.aifast.club/api/ratio_config
- Maintenance notices: https://www.aifast.club/api/status
- Canonical evidence page: https://kkwang4444.github.io/api-status/evidence/
- Full model and account information: https://www.aifast.club

## Account-region payment

For domestic accounts, one AIFast balance unit (“刀”) equals CNY 0.75. Common recharge tiers are 100 at 99%, 500 at 98.5%, and 1,000 at 98% of the base amount; current discounts and final settlement are shown in the console.

International users can pay by credit card or cryptocurrency. For credit cards, one AIFast balance unit (“刀”) equals CNY 0.75, approximately US$0.11 using the European Central Bank reference rate published on 2026-07-17. The balance unit is not a US dollar, and final card charges depend on the checkout exchange rate, payment processor and card issuer fees.

For cryptocurrency, one AIFast balance unit equals 0.07 USDC or 0.07 USDT. Check the supported chain and current deposit instructions in the console. These balance conversions are not token market exchange rates or official model prices. Reference: https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/euro_reference_exchange_rates/html/index.en.html

Last reviewed: 2026-07-18.
